prompt: Create a .NET 9 subscription management domain with states (Trial/Active/PastDue/Cancelled), plans (Basic/Pro/Enterprise), business rules (no trial downgrade, cooling-off cancellation, immediate upgrades, deferred downgrades, auto-cancel PastDue), domain service, Result<T>, xUnit + NSubstitute tests.
assertions: Value objects (Plan, Money) are separate classes — not raw strings/decimals for plan or price
Subscription entity has private setters — no public property setters
Private constructors with static factory methods returning Result<T>
Result<T> pattern used for all operations that can fail
State transitions enforced inside the entity — cannot downgrade during trial
Cancellation implements 14-day cooling-off with reactivation support
Upgrades apply immediately, downgrades deferred to end of billing period
PastDue auto-cancel after 30 days is implemented
Domain service (SubscriptionService) orchestrates — does not contain business logic that belongs in entity
NSubstitute used for infrastructure mocks, real domain objects never mocked
Tests cover all state transition rules
IDateTimeProvider or equivalent time abstraction used for testability
